'''Vergesso Base''' was an [[asteroid base]] on [[Vergesso Prime/Legends|Vergesso Prime]] owned by [[Ororo Transportation/Legends|Ororo Transportation]] and used as a [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]] by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].

==Layout==
Vergesso Base had an artificial breathable atmosphere and life support ideal for [[humanoid/Legends|humanoids]]. It followed a cycle of 1 [[standard year/Legends|standard year]] and 24 [[Standard hour/Legends|standard hours]].<ref name="guide" />

The Base was divided into several sections, each catering to a specific area of station operation. [[The Facility]] was the uppermost layer of the station, full of [[Hangar/Legends|hangars]], cargo handling and storage facilities, and repair services. [[The Life]] catered to the crews visiting the station, including quarters, [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] facilities, amusements, and [[Cantina/Legends|bars]]. The [[Base Operations Center]] situated at the center of the asteroid, was the heart of the station, and was responsible for base command and control.

It was equipped with a [[Stellar-class starport]] and [[tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]] used to manipulate companion asteroids and keep the [[Asteroid Hangar]]s hidden.<ref name="guide" />
===Defenses===
The base maintained a defensive force of a pair of escort [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigates]], ''[[Markess Narn]]'' and ''[[Childerly]]''; and several [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] including [[Gauntlet starfighter|Gauntlets]], [[Zebra starfighter|Zebras]], [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]], and [[IRD starfighter|IRD]]s (stolen from the [[Corporate_Sector_Authority/Legends|CSA]]). Visiting/stationed small [[capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] and starfighters theoretically contributed to the Base strength; specifically Rebels were ordered to defend the base in case of any attack, recognising its important position for the Alliance. The surface of the asteroid was equipped with several weapons such as turbolasers.<ref name="guide" />

[[Natori Association]] provided soldiers for internal security and defense in case of an intrusion; Natori [[platoon/Legends|platoon]]s manned the [[frigate/Legends|frigates]] and the surface defenses.<ref name="guide" />

The force was intended for pirates and rival organizations, not Imperial forces. Indeed, Ororo was bribing the [[High Commander]] of the [[Oversector/Legends|Oversector]], the [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Kintaro]], who ordered the sectorial [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|ISB]] to neglect investigating Vergesso.<ref name="guide" />

==History==
The asteroid was first used as a survey installation for Dr. [[Ecile Vergesso]] who moved there to study the system. After that project, the base was taken by a band of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], and then [[smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] used it as an exchange point, until Ororo Transportation started using it a shadowport.<ref name="guide" />

Natori, which was another Tenloss branch, except from providing security, used the base as local headquarters and mustering station.<ref name="guide" />

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] Ororo invited the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] to use its smuggling base. The Alliance was concerned about cooperating with a criminal organization; however the Alliance was desperate for any outside assistance, such as the facilities the Base provided. They agreed after they discovered only one business connection to Tenloss.<ref name="guide" />

The Alliance considered Vergesso a big asset; [[Oponni Narthrup]] acted as the general Rebel administrator in Lybeya, and official liaison with Ororo. The Alliance used the base as an allied port where they could resupply and refuel their ships; [[Rebel Alliance Special Forces|SpecForces]] bought weapons there for their missions, and it was a safe haven for Rebel [[Privateer/Legends|privateers]]. Even though the cooperation was uneasy, and the Rebels felt uncomfortable coexisting with thugs, mercenaries and [[drug dealer]]s, it saved the cost of maintaining an actual port, and the Alliance obliged itself to defend it in case of attack.<ref name="guide" />

It attracted criminals, privateers, Rebels and businessbeings from all the galaxy. Tons of illegal cargo--from stolen, plundered and black market goods to spice and bootleg holos--passed through its storehouses and were about 10% of the money exchaned on the base. Some smugglers used its storage space as drop-off point. [[spy/Legends|Spies]] and [[Information broker/Legends|Information brokers]] were selling Imperial, Alliance, corporate or local intelligence.<ref name="guide" />

[[mercenary/Legends|Mercenaries]], as well as Rebels, could find weapons there. [[Galindas Exports]] used Vergesso to supply materiel to the Rebels, hundreds of tons each [[standard month/Legends|standard month]]. Ship weapons could be upgraded at its hangars. Although Ororo didn't allow the presence of [[Tank/Legends|tanks]] or other artillery, deals for delivery could be made with contacts.<ref name="guide" />

With the best stocked spice market in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], Vergesso functioned also as an exchange point for legal and illegal spices, attracting smugglers and [[black marketeer]]s from all across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and [[spice/Legends|spice]] users from all the region bought from [[D'ring'my]].<ref name="guide" />

Not all smugglers operating on the base belonged to Ororo; those directly supplied Ororo ships with spice, mixed with legitimate cargo. About half of the smugglers though worked for other companies or were independent speculators, who sold spice at market (such as in [[The Life]]) to Ororo or other parties. Spice was shipped out either on Ororo or other smuggling ships; Ororo made even 25% more per each deal.<ref name="guide" />

[[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] [[agent/Legends|agents]] throughout the Outer Rim also ran through Vergesso in secret, both to lose any trackers, and to monitor suspected Rebel activity. Although the Grand Moff Kintaro was bribed to ignore Vergesso, Imperial Intelligence watched over the base's activities. Because of the ongoing investigation (and the base's usage as a transfer point), the [[Ubiqtorate/Legends|Ubiqtorate]] neglected reporting the base to the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Military]]<ref name="guide" /> until [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]] directed the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] against the base.

The base was used by the [[Azzameen family|Azzameens]] to transfer stolen goods to the Alliance. When [[Aeron Azzameen]] decided to stay for a "vacation," [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]] attacked the system in the [[Battle of the Bajic Shipyards]]. Aeron was able to evacuate with the help of her brother [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace]] before it was destroyed.<ref name="Shadows of the Empire">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]]</ref><ref name="X-Wing Alliance">''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]''</ref>

In the later years of the Civil War, parts of the base were repairable and eventually it passed to other hands.<ref name="guide" />
